name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.neural_networks.EstimatorQNN
summary: A quantum neural network based on the Estimator primitive that computes expectation values of observables as outputs, supporting forward passes and gradient backpropagation for variational training.
docstring: EstimatorQNN computes expectation values of a parameterized quantum circuit evaluated with an Estimator primitive. Inputs are encoded via a feature map part of the circuit; weights are the trainable parameters.
internal_keywords: _default_precision _input_gradients _org_circuit _weight_params default_precision gradients input_gradients num_samples num_virtual_qubits weight_params weights_grad
internal_comments: set gradient Prepare circuit-observable-parameter tuples (PUBs) For BaseEstimatorV2, run the estimator using PUBs and specified precision prepare parameters in the required format

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.neural_networks.SamplerQNN
summary: A quantum neural network based on the Sampler primitive that maps circuit measurement probability distributions to output values via an interpret function, enabling classification and generative tasks.
docstring: SamplerQNN samples from a parameterized quantum circuit and applies an interpret function to the resulting probability distribution.
internal_keywords: SamplerPubResult SparseArray _input_gradients _org_circuit _weight_params counts_i input_gradients interpret num_samples num_virtual_qubits probs_i total_shots weight_params weights_grad
internal_comments: Set primitive, provide default Set gradient derive target values to be used in computations This definition is required by mypy type: ignore Warn user that output_shape parameter will be ignored allocate handle spaced bit strings if any pylint: disable=invalid-name if looks like a binary string, treat as base-2 decimal string last resort SamplerV2: get per-parameter-set counts Prefer pub.data.get_counts(i); fall back to alternatives if not available. new API alternative field names some builds expose absolute fallback (aggregated; avoids crash but will degrade accuracy) normalize to probabilities keys -> ints, filter to valid range map through interpret and write ONLY row i pylint: disable=import-error get index for input or weights gradients interpret integer and construct key if key is an array-type, cast to hashable tuple store value for inputs or weights gradients we compute input gradients first pylint: disable=no-member prepare parameters in the required format type: ignore[arg-type] type: ignore[arg-type]

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.neural_networks.NeuralNetwork
summary: Abstract base class for all quantum neural networks, defining the forward and backward interface for expectation-value and sampling-based QNNs.
docstring: Base class for quantum neural networks in Qiskit Machine Learning.
internal_keywords: SparseArray _input_gradients _num_weights input_grad_reshaped input_gradients new_weight_params num_samples num_weights original_shape weight_grad weight_grad_reshaped weight_params
internal_comments: pylint: disable=too-many-positional-arguments output shape may be derived later, so check it only if it is not None there's a single value in the input. add an empty dimension for samples (batch dimension) flatten lower dimensions, keep num_inputs as a last dimension no input, no weights, just execute circuit once As the data (parameter values) for the primitive is ordered as inputs followed by weights we need to ensure that the parameters are ordered like this naturally too so the rewrites parameters to ensure this. "inputs" as a name comes before "weights" and within they are numerically ordered.

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.neural_networks.EffectiveDimension
summary: Computes the global effective dimension of a quantum neural network as an information-geometric measure of the model's expressive power and trainability.
docstring: EffectiveDimension evaluates the Fisher information matrix of a QNN to compute the effective dimension.
internal_keywords: NeuralNetwork _input_samples _num_input_samples _num_weight_samples _weight_samples backward_pass dataset_size dets_div effective_dimensions effective_dims f_mod fisher_avg fisher_information fisher_trace forward_pass grad_vectors gradients input_samples logsum_axis model_outputs n_expanded normalized_fisher one_plus_fmod t_after_backward t_after_forward t_backward t_before_forward t_forward weight_samples
internal_comments: Store arguments Setup things for weight and input samples via setters that deal with the union of types that can be passed so that the private vars above that have just been set with temp values of right types to establish typing, get the right values per what was passed. Note, the samples ones above had been set to None but this results in errors when checking using mypy 1.16.0 random sampling from uniform distribution to be sure we have an array random sampling from normal distribution to be sure we have an array post-processing in the case of EstimatorQNN output, to match the SamplerQNN output format add dimension to model outputs for broadcasting get grad-vectors (gradient_k/model_output_k) multiply by sqrt(model_output) so that the outer product cross term is correct after Einstein summation We clip to 0 and mask zero entries to avoid divide-by-zero warnings. compute the sum of matrices obtained from outer product of grad-vectors compute the trace with all normalized_fisher average the normalized_fisher over the num_input_samples to get the empirical normalized_fisher calculate normalized_normalized_fisher for all the empirical normalized_fisher expand dims for broadcasting calculate effective dimension for each data sample size out of normalized normalized_fisher take log. of the determinant because of overflow divide by 2 because of square root step 1: Monte Carlo sampling step 2: compute as many fisher info. matrices as (input, params) sets step 3: get normalized fisher info matrices step 4: compute eff. dim

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.neural_networks.LocalEffectiveDimension
summary: Computes the local effective dimension of a quantum neural network around specific weight values, measuring model capacity at a given parameter point.
docstring: LocalEffectiveDimension evaluates the effective dimension of a QNN at specified parameter values.
internal_keywords: _num_weight_samples _weight_samples weight_samples
internal_comments: override setter to enforce 1 set of parameters random sampling from uniform distribution there is a weird mypy error if we keep the same variable name, so there's 'weights' additional check to accept 1D arrays

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.algorithms.classifiers.NeuralNetworkClassifier

For user checking and validation. pylint: disable=invalid-name mypy definition Binary classification Multi-class classification Convert class indices to one-hot encoded format Assumes an activation function is applied within the forward method our math does not work with sparse arrays cast is required by mypy cast is required by mypy string data is assumed to be categorical OneHotEncoder expects data with shape (n_samples, n_features) but LabelEncoder expects shape (n_samples,) so set desired shape the model is being trained, fit first conversion to one hot of the labels is required the model is being trained, fit first

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.connectors.TorchConnector
summary: Bridges a Qiskit NeuralNetwork to PyTorch by wrapping it as a torch.nn.Module, enabling hybrid quantum-classical training with PyTorch optimizers and autograd.
docstring: TorchConnector makes a QNN available as a PyTorch module for use in hybrid classical-quantum neural network architectures.
internal_keywords: NeuralNetwork _neural_network initial_weights neural_network weight_param
internal_comments: connector is sparse while the underlying neural network is not Register param. in graph following PyTorch naming convention If `weight_param` is assigned to `self._weights` after registration, it will not be re-registered, and we can keep the private var. name "_weights" for compatibility. The alternative, doing: `self._weights = TorchParam(Tensor(neural_network.num_weights))` would register the parameter with the name "_weights".

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.gradients.SPSAEstimatorGradient
summary: Estimates expectation value gradients using the Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation method, a noise-robust alternative to parameter-shift for noisy hardware.

Combine inputs into a single job to reduce overhead. Prepare circuit-observable-parameter tuples (PUBs) For BaseEstimatorV2, run the estimator using PUBs and specified precision Compute the gradients. Calculate the gradient for each batch. Note that (``diff`` / ``offset``) is the gradient since ``offset`` is a perturbation vector of 1s and -1s. Take the average of the batch gradients.

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.gradients.QFI
summary: Computes the Quantum Fisher Information matrix of a parameterized quantum circuit, used to characterize the trainability and natural gradient of variational algorithms.
docstring: QFI computes the quantum Fisher information matrix for a parameterized quantum circuit.
internal_keywords: AlgorithmJob BaseQGT QFIResult derivative_type precision temp_derivative_type
internal_comments: Allow a single circuit to be passed in. If parameters is None, we calculate the gradients of all parameters in each circuit. If parameters is not None, we calculate the gradients of the specified parameters. None in parameters means that the gradients of all parameters in the corresponding circuit are calculated. May still be None Set the derivative type to real

name: /qiskit_machine_learning/qiskit_machine_learning.gradients.LinCombQGT
summary: Computes the Quantum Geometric Tensor using the linear combination of unitaries method, providing curvature information for Riemannian optimization of variational circuits.
docstring: LinCombQGT computes the quantum geometric tensor which generalizes the QFI.
internal_keywords: DerivativeType QGTResult SUPPORTED_GATES _lin_comb_qgt_circuit_cache derivative_type has_transformed_precision lin_comb_qgt_circuits observable_1 observable_2 partial_sum_n phase_fix phase_fix_job phase_fix_obs phase_fixes precision qgt_circuits
internal_comments: Prepare circuits for the gradient of the specified parameters. Compute the first term in the QGT generate the all of the circuits for the first term in the QGT and cache them. Only the circuit related to specified parameters will be executed. In the future, we can generate the specified circuits on demand. Run the single job with all circuits. Compute the second term in the QGT if phase fix is enabled. Compute the phase fix Select the real or imaginary part of the phase fix if needed Compute the QGT type: ignore Compute the first term in the QGT Add the conjugate of the upper triangle to the lower triangle Subtract the phase fix from the QGT type: ignore
